BOROGAN, a town of Turkey in Europe, in the prov. of Wallachia, 43 m. E of Bukarest.
BOROGNE LAKE. See BORGNE.
BOROJO, a town of Venezuela, in the intendancy and 70 m. WSW of Coro, on a small river which discharges itself into the gulf of Maracaibo.
BOROKHOTAN, a town of Mongolia, in the prov. of Kharot, on the r. bank of the Tchoro, near the E base of the Kingan mountains, 45 m. NNE of Barin, and 280 m. NNE of Pekin.

BOROROS, a district of SE Africa, stretching from the Zambesi, on the SW, to the Mozambique channel on the SE, and extending northwards to Lake Maravi. It is inhabited by several barbarous tribes, of whom the Massi, Maravi, and Ruengas, are the principal; but it is still to a great extent unknown.

BOROROS, a district of Brazil, in the prov. of Matto Grosso, stretching from the Araguaya on the W, to the S. Lourenzo or Cuayba on the E. It is intersected by several lofty mountain ridges, and is inhabited by a numerous aboriginal tribe of the same name.
BOROS JENO, a town of Hungary, cap. of the com. of Arad, and 30 m. NE of Alt Arad, on the S bank of the Feher-koros. It is the residence of a Greek protopape, and contains several churches.
BOROS SEBES, a town of Hungary, in the com. of Arad, and 40 m. ENE of Alt Arad, on a tributary of the Feher-koros.
BOROSTYANKO. See Bernstein.

BOROTALA, a river of N Asia, in Sungana, which issues from a small lake on the ENE side of Mount Talki, curves to the E, and falls into the Kaltaroshke or Boulkatsi Nor, called also in the Russian maps Borotala. The name of this river signifies, in the Kalmuk tongue, 'Grey plain.'
BOROTKOVA, a town of Russia in Asia, in the gov. and 330 m. NNW of Yeniseisk, on the I. bank of the Yenisei.

BOROUGH, a hamlet in the p. of Llanelly, co. of Carmarthen, 14 m. SE of Carmarthen, on the N bank of the Burry. The inhabitants are chiefly miners and sailors. Excellent coal is wrought here. Pop. in 1821, 2,621; in 1831, 4,173; in 1841, 6,846.

BOROUGHBRIDGE, a market-town and chapelry in the p. of Aldborough, W. R. of Yorkshire; 206 m. NNW of London, and 17 m. NW of York, on the great northern road. Area of chapelry, 220 acres. Pop. in 1801, 680; in 1841, 1,024. The town stands on the S bank of the Ure, which is here navigable. It was formerly a borough returning two members to parliament. The right of election, previous to its disfranchisement by the Reform bill, was held by about 74 individuals, burgage-tenants, wholly under the influence of the duke of Newcastle. B. derives its name from a bridge over the Ure, erected soon after the Conquest. In the market-place, near the centre of the town, is a very elevated cross formed of eight columns united with a foliaged capital. Races are held annually in the vicinity. About half-a-mile to the S of the town are three rude monumental pillars, called the Devil's Arrows, approaching somewhat to the form of obelisks, and measuring, respectively, 18, 22, and 31 ft. in height. They are composed of the common coarse rag-stone. "Here was, in the British times," observes Dr. Stukeley, "the great Panegyre of the Druids, the midsummer meeting of all the country round, to celebrate the great quarterly sacrifice; accompanied with games, sports, races, and all kinds of exercises, with universal festivity. This was like the Panathenaan, the Olympian, Nemæan meetings and games among the Grecians. These obelisks were as the metæ of the races; the remembrance hereof is transmitted in the great fair held here on St. Barnabas'-day." By Dr. Stillingfleet they are regarded as ancient British deities, | while Camden, Leland, and Drake, suppose them to have been the work of the Romans.—Also a chapelry in the parishes of Ling, Othery, Middlezoy, and West Zoyland, Somersetshire; 5 m. NW of Langport, on the E bank of the Parrot, which is here navigable, and in the neighbourhood of the Bristol and Exeter railway. Pop. 93.
BOROUGH-FEN (VILLE), an extra-parochial district in the liberty of Peterborough, co. of Northampton; 5 m. NNE of Peterborough. Area 3,130 acres. Pop. in 1801, 116; in 1841, 192.
BOROUGH-GREEN, a hamlet in the p. of Wrotham, Kent, 61 m. W of Maidstone.
BOROUNY, a town of Russia in Europe, in the gov. and 45 m. SE of Vilna, in the dist. and 12 m. SE of Ochmani, near the r. bank of the Peresina.
BOROVIA, a town of Spain, in Old Castile, in the prov. and 32 m. ESE of Soria, on the SE side of the Sierra-Moncayo.
